title: chargeback — marketing events (negative-value + fraud signal)
description: Marketing-lane events fired by the chargeback module — every ChargebackLost fires a downstream marketing::ChargebackFiled NEGATIVE-VALUE CONVERSION + FRAUD SIGNAL event. Ad networks subtract the disputed amount from the ad campaign's revenue attribution (ROAS adjustment) + retrain fraud models MORE HEAVILY than on refunds (chargebacks are a stronger fraud correlation signal) + adjust bidding on customer segments with high chargeback rates. Payload includes the original conversion's ad_network + campaign_id (looked up via the payment's linked marketing_event) + the reason_category so the ad network can distinguish fraud-labeled chargebacks (aggressive fraud model retraining) from consumer_disputes-labeled chargebacks (product-quality signal, adjust bidding gently). Consent tier is `advertising` — customers who have withdrawn advertising consent have their chargeback reversals suppressed at dispatch. Chargeback still lands internally; only the marketing lane skips.

events.name: Chargeback Filed
properties: reversed_amount_cents (same as amount_cents; explicit for provider APIs that require this shape)
network (card network — visa / mastercard / amex / etc.)
reason_code (network-native code — for provider APIs that consume specific codes)
fraud_signal (boolean — true when reason_category='fraud'; drives ad-network fraud model retraining aggression)
events.description: Fires on ChargebackLost. Distinct from the refund module's 'Refund Issued' event — refunds are merchant-initiated; chargebacks are bank-forced. Ad networks correlate chargebacks with click IDs to identify fraud-prone traffic sources + retrain fraud models. Fraud model retraining is MORE aggressive for chargebacks than for refunds because chargebacks are a stronger fraud signal (customer disputed unauthorized use; refund can be a genuine dissatisfaction). The reason_category field distinguishes fraud-labeled chargebacks (aggressive fraud model retraining) from consumer_disputes-labeled chargebacks (product-quality signal, adjust bidding gently but do not aggressively re-train fraud models).

events.name: Chargeback Expired
properties: was_expired (true — distinguishes auto-loss from bank-decided loss)
events.description: Distinct from 'Chargeback Filed' — this fires on ChargebackExpired (auto-loss via missed evidence deadline). was_expired=true property distinguishes for the ad-network's downstream analysis. Some networks weight was_expired losses less heavily in fraud model retraining (auto-loss may not indicate fraud — could be operational failure).

delivery_semantics.retry_policy: exponential backoff (10s / 60s / 5m / 30m / 3h / 24h) up to 6 attempts; then dead-letter queue for manual replay
delivery_semantics.circuit_breaker: per-provider circuit breaker in the marketing module; if one provider is down, the others still receive events
delivery_semantics.ledger: growth::marketing owns the two-table ledger: marketing_events (canonical) + marketing_deliveries (per-provider-per-attempt log)

pii_hashing.user_id: sent as external_id (unhashed) — matches ad-network's advanced-matching contract

notes: This is the MARKETING lane per growth-and-observability.md — different consent tier (`advertising`, subset of marketing) than the analytics lane (`essential` for ChargebackLost).
Ad networks require SHA-256 hashing of PII per their contracts — the growth::marketing module handles hashing, this module just declares which properties are PII.

Reversal events with fraud signals are LOAD-BEARING for ad-network fraud model quality — chargebacks are the strongest correlation signal ad networks use to identify fraudulent traffic sources. Without this feed, ad networks continue bidding on fraud-prone segments.

The marketing event lookup uses payment.metadata.marketing_conversion_event_id (populated when the parent payment fired a conversion event at capture time). Payments without a linked marketing conversion event do NOT fire the marketing lane — the chargeback still lands internally.

Consent-gate: `advertising` tier. Customers who have withdrawn advertising consent have their chargeback marketing events suppressed at dispatch. The chargeback itself still lands + finalization chain fires; only the marketing lane skips.

For coupon-cascaded reversals, the coupon module fires its OWN marketing reversal event with clawback_reason='chargeback' — the two events are distinct + additive at the ad-network side. Meta's dedup is via event_id in the payload; the marketing module ensures unique event_ids per module + chargeback + coupon.

Fraud model retraining aggression is provider-specific + configured in growth::marketing. Meta CAPI accepts a fraud_flagged flag on Purchase events; Google Ads EC uses a custom parameter; TikTok/Microsoft/Snapchat do not accept a fraud flag but still consume the reversal for ROAS adjustment.
